title: We built AI that analyzes trading signals — not one that spins them
author: ExplanationNormal339
selftext:
Hey folks,  
I’m one of the devs behind **AimyTrade.io**. We didn’t want to build another “black box buys for you” type bot. Instead, we made something a little different:

For any stock or crypto ticker, our AI:

* Runs real-time technical indicator analysis (RSI, MACD, volume)
* Detects signal opportunities (with confidence scores)
* Suggests entry, exit, stop, and target ranges
* Combines risk assessment to flag when a signal is weaker or riskier

It’s meant to *augment* your trading mind, not replace it.  
We’d love input from this community:

* Which signals do you trust (momentum, reversal, volume breakouts)?
* How do you validate AI-based suggestions?
* What would you want in a “confidence metric” that actually matters?

Not here to sell — just want feedback from people who understand the balance between model and market.

comments.author: Hour-Journalist7328
comments.body: Volume profile, cummulative volume delta are the ones I trust the most. I only trust AI if i know what data they trained it from, their trade tactic … conf metric is more orless depends on how the AI was made and the regime of the market.

replies.author: ExplanationNormal339
replies.body:
Yeah makes sense. CVD and volume profile are usually more reliable than indicators alone.
AimyTrade looks for setups where RSI, MACD and volume flow align with high probability patterns from past data.
Confidence comes from how similar current conditions are to those setups.
We retrain monthly since static models fall apart when market regimes change.
How do you use delta divergence in your own strategy?

comments.author: hanslandar
comments.body: trend line breaks and bounces , in combination with volume rising, as well as support and resistance levels and chart patterns. i know, they are hard to automate compared to RSI and so on, but i think they are the most reliable

comments.author: Flashy-Distance4520
comments.body: This is a great trade assistant. Built something similar to this and I have been profitable. Good job.
